questions.question_text: A property owner sells their own home without using a real estate agent. Does this activity require a Kansas real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners selling their own property are exempt
distractors: Yes, all real estate sales require a license
Only if the sale price exceeds $100,000
Only if the property is commercial
questions.explanation: Property owners dealing with their own real estate are exempt from licensing requirements. A license is required when someone lists, sells, buys, or leases real estate for another person for compensation.

questions.question_text: An attorney negotiates a real estate transaction as part of their legal representation of a client. Is a real estate license required?
questions.correct_answer: No, attorneys in their practice are exempt
distractors: Yes, all negotiations require a license
Only if they receive a separate commission
Only if they are representing the buyer
questions.explanation: Attorneys acting within the scope of their law practice are exempt from real estate licensing requirements. This does not extend to acting as a real estate broker outside their legal duties.

questions.question_text: Which activity would require a Kansas real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing properties for others for a fee
distractors: Selling your own residential property
Managing your own rental apartments
Appraising property as a licensed appraiser
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, or leasing real estate for others for compensation requires a Kansas real estate license. Personal property transactions and licensed appraising are exempt.

questions.question_text: A court appoints a person to manage the sale of a deceased person's property. Does this person need a real estate license?
distractors: Yes, all property sales need a licensee
Only if the estate exceeds $50,000
Only if they charge a management fee
questions.explanation: Court-appointed fiduciaries (such as executors, administrators, and trustees) acting under court authority are exempt from Kansas real estate licensing requirements.

questions.question_text: A friend offers to help find a buyer for a neighbor's home in exchange for a $500 finder's fee. What does Kansas law require?
questions.correct_answer: A real estate license is required for this activity
distractors: No license needed for finder's fees under $1,000
No license needed between acquaintances
Only a referral certificate is needed
questions.explanation: Any person who helps buy, sell, or lease real estate for another for compensation must hold a Kansas real estate license, regardless of the amount or relationship between parties.

questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education must a Kansas salesperson candidate complete?
distractors: 30 hours
90 hours
120 hours
questions.explanation: Kansas requires 60 hours of pre-license education: a 30-hour Principles of Real Estate course plus a 30-hour Kansas Practice course. Both must be KREC-approved.

questions.question_text: A candidate completes the Principles of Real Estate course in January. By when must they take the licensing exam?
distractors: Within 6 months of completion
Within 24 months of completion
Within 90 days of completion
questions.explanation: Kansas requires candidates to take the licensing exam within 12 months of completing the Principles of Real Estate course. After that, the course certificate expires.

questions.question_text: What passing score is required on each section of the Kansas real estate exam?
distractors: 75% on both sections separately
60% on state and 70% on national
80% combined across both sections
questions.explanation: Candidates must score at least 70% on both the national section (56/80) and the state section (21/30). Passing one section does not carry over if the other is failed.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is required as part of the Kansas salesperson license application?
distractors: Three letters of recommendation
A minimum of one year of experience
Completion of a post-license internship
questions.explanation: Kansas requires all license applicants to undergo a background check including fingerprinting. There is no experience requirement or internship for salesperson candidates.

questions.question_text: The Kansas salesperson pre-license education consists of which two courses?
questions.correct_answer: Principles of Real Estate and Kansas Practice
distractors: Real Estate Law and Kansas Practice
Principles and Real Estate Finance
Kansas Law and Brokerage Operations
questions.explanation: Kansas requires two 30-hour KREC-approved courses: Principles of Real Estate (30 hours) and Kansas Practice (30 hours), totaling 60 hours of pre-license education.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate passes the licensing exam but has not yet found a sponsoring broker. Can KREC issue the license?
questions.correct_answer: No, a sponsoring broker must be named first
distractors: Yes, the license is issued as inactive
Yes, with a 90-day grace period to find one
Yes, as a provisional license for six months
questions.explanation: Kansas does not issue inactive salesperson licenses. A candidate must have a sponsoring broker before KREC will issue the license. This is unique to Kansas compared to some other states.

questions.question_text: Under Kansas law, a salesperson must perform all licensed activities under whose supervision?
distractors: Any licensed Kansas broker
The KREC Executive Director
A designated team leader
questions.explanation: A Kansas salesperson must work under the supervision of their specific sponsoring broker. All licensed activities must be conducted through and under that broker's oversight.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to transfer from Broker A to Broker B. What must happen regarding their license?
questions.correct_answer: The transfer must be reported to KREC
distractors: The salesperson must retake the state exam
KREC must approve the transfer in advance
The salesperson must complete additional CE
questions.explanation: When a salesperson changes brokers, the change must be reported to KREC. The salesperson does not need to retake the exam or complete additional education for a simple broker transfer.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes Kansas from many other states regarding salesperson license status?
questions.correct_answer: Kansas does not allow inactive salesperson licenses
distractors: Kansas requires two sponsoring brokers
Kansas issues provisional licenses first
Kansas requires broker approval for all listings
questions.explanation: Unlike many states that allow salesperson licenses to be placed on inactive status, Kansas requires an active broker affiliation before a salesperson license can be issued.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's sponsoring broker retires and closes the brokerage. What happens to the salesperson's license?
questions.correct_answer: The license is deactivated until a new broker is found
distractors: The license is automatically revoked by KREC
The license converts to an independent broker license
The license remains active for a 90-day grace period
questions.explanation: When a salesperson loses their broker affiliation, the license is deactivated. The salesperson must find a new sponsoring broker and notify KREC to reactivate the license.
